human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for information product analytics specificity, product and content metric rigor, source lineage, confidentiality handling, client and content workflow approval awareness, unsupported-claim refusal, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to information product analytics, metric-definition logic, product and content reporting, source reconciliation, caveat design, confidentiality handling, and signoff-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover ISO/IEC/IEEE software measurement process, SEC KPI guidance, W3C data best practices, W3C data quality vocabulary, W3C provenance ontology, MRC digital audience measurement standards, NIST Privacy Framework, NIST SP 800-53, FTC advertising claim guidance, and FTC personal-information guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, ambiguous metric definitions, stale or incomplete source data, conflicting published metrics, unsupported causal and policy claims, client and content workflow handling, confidentiality restrictions, adjacent specialist boundaries, unsupported benchmark conclusions, terminology normalization, missing tenant facts, and tenant-specific adaptation with policy conflict.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/information-software-and-digital-media/information-product-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent legal, editorial, QA, delivery management, account, product management, data governance, privacy, security, finance, and contract lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated information product analytics specialist for product, subscriber, content, experiment, support, release, and client-delivery analytics with source reconciliation, caveat handling, confidentiality-aware delivery, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
